titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Grover Francis Powers Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Scott Libson

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 08:53

did.abstract: The papers of Grover Francis Powers contain correspondence, reports, writings, and a scrapbook. They document Powers' interest in mentally challenged children and involvement with the National Association for Retarded Children, the American Pediatric Society, and the Southbury Training School, as well as other organizations.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Yale University Historical Medical Library, 1980.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Grover Francis Powers Papers (MS 1334). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Grover Francis Powers was born in Colfax, Indiana, in 1887. He graduated from Purdue University in 1908 and from Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine in 1913. Following graduation, Powers remained in Baltimore, Maryland, as a resident pediatrician. In 1921, he was invited to the Yale University School of Medicine, where he would remain until his retirement in 1952. Between 1927 and 1952, he served as chairman of the department of pediatrics. Powers was instrumental in the foundation of the Southbury Training School for mentally retarded children. He also served as president of the American Pediatric Society and as chairman of the scientific advisory board of the National Association of Retarded Children. Powers was one of the first to propose humane treatment of the mentally challenged. He died on April 18, 1967.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers of Grover Francis Powers contain correspondence, reports, writings, and a scrapbook. They document Powers' interest in mentally challenged children and involvement with the National Association of Retarded Children, the American Pediatric Society, and Southbury Training School, as well as other organizations.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: American Pediatric Society [mailings and pamphlets related to annual meetings]
American Academy of Pediatrics Committee on Medical Education
"Ad Hoc Committee to Study the Need for a Children's Medical Center as an Integral Part of The Johns Hopkins Medical Institutions" [report of findings, draft of report, and minutes]
Darrow, Daniel Cady and James Lawder Gamble [correspondence, articles, and documents relating to Powers' nomination of Darrow and Gamble for the Nobel Prize]
"State of Connecticut Project Proposal for Mental Retardation Planning Grant"
Association for Retarded Children in Greater New Haven
"Prospectus & Building Program for a New Training School for Mental Defectives at Southbury, Connecticut"
Printed materials, handbook, brochure, and clipping

"Notes submitted by Medical members to The Commission to Study Laws and Facilities of Connecticut Pertaining to the Prevention, Care and Treatment of Mental Disease and Defect and Allied Problems, and to Make Recommendations Thereon"
"Prospectus and Building Program for a New Training School for Mental Defectives at Southbury, Connecticut"
"Proposal for a Center for Research in Maternal and Child Health and in Human Development at the Albert Einstein College of Medicine-Bronx Municipal Hospital Center"
Research materials, source materials, minutes, reports, and correspondence
"Directory of NARC Committees and Research Advisory Board"
"Executive Committee of SRAB [Scientific Research Advisory Board] Meeting"
Reports of Scientific Research Advisory Board
"Coeliac Disease," by Powers and Daniel C. Darrow
Correspondence and non-professional writings [also contains some clippings and photographs]
